Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(5 comments)

Thanks for the patch.  A few minor improvements possible.

Also, this should get a new test case to verify this functionality, probably sanity.sh test_56wa() or would be a good place for it.

Patch Set 1:

(8 comments)

Now that the 2.10 feature landing window is open again it would be great if this patch was refreshed.

Please include a test case in sanity.sh that tests out the different cases (e.g. autostripe by size, limit size per OST, skip full OSTs, etc.).

Patch Set 2:

FYI, colleagues at NASA pointed out that the log function curved the wrong way.   "sqrt($SIZE_IN_GB)" would probably be a better fit for the initial auto stripe algorithm.

Sorry I haven't had time to work on this.  Thanks to Steve G. for picking it up!

Patch Set 9:

Hi Nathan,

Is there any rationale for using sqrt() + 1?

Patch Set 9:

John,

sqrt()+1 was a simple yet relatively good fit for what I found as "best practices" published by various sites. (ORNL, ERDC, NERSC, U. Tennessee, NASA)  But it has the advantage of being continuous rather than step function.  Attempts to strike a balance between small file metadata efficiency, medium file performance, and large file OST capacity balancing.

I believe it would be a definite improvement to just add the arg now.  Then in the future it could be revisited in light of PFL, DOM, and maybe even add a plugin type capability or admin-defined algorithm.

-Nathan

commit.subject: LU-8207 scripts: add auto-stripe option to lfs_migrate
commit.message:
LU-8207 scripts: add auto-stripe option to lfs_migrate

Add a "-A" flag to lfs_migrate, which will automatically select the
stripe count as the file is rewritten. Initial algorithm to
determine stripe count is sqrt(size_in_GB)+1, with an additional cap
on object size, though the algorithm or thresholds could conceivably
change in the future.  The primary intent for this feature is to be
able to give users a tool to fix stripe settings on existing files
based on file size.

A new "-C" flag specifies the object size cap.  On each OST, the
amount of space available for migration is capped by dividing the
free space of the smallest OST by the specified value.

A new "-M" flag allows OSTs with free space less than the specified
value to be considered unavailable for migration.

A new "-v" flag increases verbosity to help debug what is being done.

A new "-X" flag limits the amount of free space on each OST that
can be used for migration to the specified value.  This flag is
useful for testing by simulating OSTs that are nearly full.

A new sanity test verifies the operation of the new "-A" flag.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Nathan Dauchy <nathan.dauchy@nasa.gov>
Signed-off-by: Steve Guminski <stephenx.guminski@intel.com>
